name: CHEERS 2022
description: Consolidated Health Economic Evaluation Reporting Standards 2022.

items.title: Title
items.description: Identify the study as an economic evaluation and specify the interventions being compared.

items.title: Abstract
items.description: Provide a structured summary that highlights context, key methods, results, and alternative analyses.

items.title: Background and objectives
items.description: Give the context for the study, the study question, and its practical relevance for decision-making in policy or practice.

items.title: Health economic analysis plan
items.description: Indicate whether a health economic analysis plan was developed and where available.

items.title: Study population
items.description: Describe characteristics of the study population (such as age range, demographics, socioeconomic, or clinical characteristics).

items.title: Setting and location
items.description: Provide relevant contextual information that may influence findings.

items.title: Comparators
items.description: Describe the interventions or strategies being compared and why chosen.

items.title: Perspective
items.description: State the perspective(s) adopted by the study and why chosen.

items.title: Time horizon
items.description: State the time horizon for the study and why appropriate.

items.title: Discount rate
items.description: Report the discount rate(s) and reason chosen.

items.title: Selection of outcomes
items.description: Describe what outcomes were used as the measure(s) of benefit(s) and harm(s).

items.title: Measurement of outcomes
items.description: Describe how outcomes used to capture benefit(s) and harm(s) were measured.

items.title: Valuation of outcomes
items.description: Describe the population and methods used to measure and value outcomes.

items.title: Measurement and valuation of resources and costs
items.description: Describe how costs were valued.

items.title: Currency, price date, and conversion
items.description: Report the dates of the estimated resource quantities and unit costs, plus the currency and year of conversion.

items.title: Rationale and description of model
items.description: If modelling is used, describe in detail and why used; report if the model is publicly available and where it can be accessed.

items.title: Analytics and assumptions
items.description: Describe any methods for analyses or statistical analyses; describe any structural or other assumptions underpinning the analysis.

items.title: Characterising heterogeneity
items.description: Describe any methods used for estimating how the results of the study vary for sub-groups.

items.title: Characterising distributional effects
items.description: Describe how impacts are distributed across different individuals or adjustments made to reflect priority populations.

items.title: Characterising uncertainty
items.description: Describe methods for characterising any sources of uncertainty.

items.title: Approach to engagement with patients and others
items.description: Describe any approaches to engage patients or service recipients, the general public, communities, or stakeholders in the design.

items.title: Study parameters
items.description: Report the values, ranges, references, and probability distributions for all parameters.

items.title: Summary of main results
items.description: Report the mean values for the main categories of costs and outcomes of interest and summarise them in the most appropriate overall measure.

items.title: Effect of uncertainty
items.description: Describe how uncertainty about analytic judgments, inputs, or projections affect findings.

items.title: Effect of engagement with patients and others
items.description: Report on any difference patient/service recipient, general public, community, or stakeholder engagement made.

items.title: Study findings, limitations, generalisability, and current knowledge
items.description: Report key findings, limitations, ethical or equity considerations, and how patients/the public could benefit.

items.title: Funding and conflicts of interest
items.description: Describe how the study was funded and any role of the funder; identify any conflicts of interest.

items.title: Data availability
items.description: Describe to what extent data, code, or other materials underlying the analysis are publicly available.
